# Vendor Checksums

This document lists every file vendored under `vendor/` along with the SHA-256
hash recorded at vendoring time. `scripts/verify-vendor.mjs` recomputes the
hashes on every build (via `npm run verify-vendor`, chained into `prebuild`)
and aborts if any file diverges from the manifest below — including extra
files lacking a recorded hash, or recorded hashes pointing at missing files.

The fenced code block at the bottom of this file is parsed by the verify
script. Keep the format compatible with `sha256sum -c` (two spaces between
hash and path).

## Files

### `vendor/fixup.js`

Patched copy of `https://www.w3.org/scripts/TR/2021/fixup.js` (downloaded
2026-05-21, upstream 429 lines). The deprecation-warning XHR block was
removed; see [fixup.js.PATCH.md](fixup.js.PATCH.md) for the exact diff and
re-derivation steps.

### `vendor/base.css`

Verbatim copy of `https://www.w3.org/StyleSheets/TR/2021/base.css`
(downloaded 2026-05-21). Used as the spec's base stylesheet, served from
the same origin so the deployed `valos-spec.html` has no runtime W3C
dependency.

Re-verify against upstream:

```sh
curl -sS -o /tmp/base.css https://www.w3.org/StyleSheets/TR/2021/base.css
diff /tmp/base.css vendor/base.css
```

### `vendor/webflow/jquery-3.5.1.min.js`

Verbatim copy of `https://d3e54v103j8qbb.cloudfront.net/js/jquery-3.5.1.min.dc5e7f18c8.js`
(downloaded 2026-05-21 from the URL embedded in the Webflow export). At
download time the file's SHA-256 matched the SRI hash on Webflow's original
`<script integrity="sha256-9/aliU8dGd2tb6OSsuzixeV4y/faTqgFtohetphbbj0=">`
attribute, confirming bit-identical bytes to what Webflow shipped. That same
hash is recorded below and re-verified on every build.

`process-webflow.mjs` rewrites the original CDN `<script>` tag to load this
local copy and **drops** the runtime `integrity`/`crossorigin` attributes:
on a same-origin file SRI triggers CORS-mode fetching that fails on `file://`
previews, and it is redundant here because integrity is anchored by the
build-time `verify-vendor.mjs` check against the hash below (which fails the
build on any mismatch) rather than by a runtime SRI attribute.

Version 3.5.1 is intentionally pinned to match Webflow's `webflow.js`
runtime; do not bump without coordinating a Webflow re-export and a
compatibility test.

### `vendor/webflow/fonts.css`

CSS generated by fetching `https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=DM+Mono:wght@300;400;500;600;700&family=Host+Grotesk:wght@300;400;500;600;700&display=swap`
with a modern Chrome user agent on 2026-05-21, then rewriting every
`url(https://fonts.gstatic.com/s/<family>/v<n>/<file>.woff2)` to
`url(./fonts/<file>.woff2)`. The woff2 files under `vendor/webflow/fonts/`
are verbatim copies of the upstream Google Fonts binaries.

Re-derive:

```sh
UA='Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36'
curl -sS -A "$UA" 'https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=DM+Mono:wght@300;400;500;600;700&family=Host+Grotesk:wght@300;400;500;600;700&display=swap' \
  | sed 's|https://fonts.gstatic.com/s/[a-z]*/v[0-9]*/|./fonts/|g' > /tmp/fonts.css
diff /tmp/fonts.css vendor/webflow/fonts.css
```

Note: Google may bump font file versions (e.g. `v16` → `v17`) over time,
which changes the woff2 URLs and contents. A diff against the current
upstream output is therefore not always exact — a hash mismatch in CI
means either tampering or an intentional re-vendor.

### `vendor/webflow/fonts/*.woff2` (8 files)

Verbatim copies of the woff2 binaries that Google Fonts referenced from the
generated `fonts.css` at vendoring time. Each filename matches the upstream
basename.

```sha256sum
9964608a849396bd00c4bfd7034afe03486469dcf20b4f6b8cbdfdd310369951  vendor/webflow/fonts/aFTR7PB1QTsUX8KYvumzEYOtbQ.woff2

```

Updating the manifest: if a vendored file changes intentionally (e.g.
re-derived from upstream, or fixup.js's patch re-applied to a new version),
run `node scripts/verify-vendor.mjs --update` to regenerate the block above,
then commit `vendor/CHECKSUMS.md` alongside the changed file.
